document.title: Risk Management Plan for ADD Digital flat panel X-ray detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan A-TCF-5-036 Rev.2 for ADD, a digital flat panel X-ray detector.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the risk management process, responsibilities, lifecycle activities, risk acceptance criteria, and post-production information handling for ADD.
keyPoints: Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
The scope applies to the entire life cycle of ADD, model name ADD.
ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
All risks are to be reduced as far as possible without economic considerations.
Risk level is calculated as Severity x Probability, with 1-4 acceptable and 5-25 unacceptable.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: The purpose of risk management plan is applied EN 60601-1 3.1 Ed. according to EN ISO 14971:2019 by establish risk management process.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Post-production information
concepts.evidence: Post-production Information should be collected through the collection methods such as surveys, customer satisfaction surveys, customer complaints (Internet, telephone, etc.), or articles or news reports.

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk risk remaining after risk control measures have been taken

evidence.claim: The document is Risk Management Plan A-TCF-5-036 Rev.2 for ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for ADD Digital flat panel X-ray detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-036 (Rev.2)

evidence.claim: Revision 2 is dated Dec 01, 2022 and was made according to updated applicable standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 2 Dec 01, 2022 Revision according to the update of applicable standards.
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Plan A-TCF-5-036 revision history

evidence.claim: The plan applies to the entire lifecycle of ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: We shall apply to entire life cycle of the following product. Product Name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector Model name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is a digital flat panel X-ray detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The risk management policy is to identify and control hazards and risks caused by the product.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the EN ISO 14971:2019 international standards of risk management we will be identified in advance and to control Hazard and Risk which may be caused by the product.

evidence.claim: Risk management is performed through manufacture, testing, installation, management, development, and production.
evidence.evidenceText: we will perform the risk management activities throughout the entire process Manufacture, testing, installation and management, development of the production.

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable national or regional regulations, relevant international standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns

evidence.claim: All risks are to be reduced as far as possible without economic considerations.
evidence.evidenceText: All risks reduced “as far as possible” without economic considerations.

evidence.claim: Risk level is Severity multiplied by Probability.
Risk levels 1 through 4 are acceptable, and risk levels 5 through 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from internal and external sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of Post production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: Internal post-production information includes PMCF, CAPA-related reports, customer complaints, service records, and customer satisfaction surveys.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints (4) A / S recorded and maintained the information obtained during maintenance (5) Customer satisfaction surveys

evidence.claim: External post-production information includes medical incident reporting, academic papers, expert opinions, similar device information, legal and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: External (1) Reporting medical incidents (Recall, the side effects reported cases of domestic and foreign) (2) Related academic papers and expert opinions (3) Information related similar medical device (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards (5) In the process of performing clinical trials information
